title: Femoral Vein Cannulation Protocol
author: Jennifer Rutkowsky
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zavf2e6

steps.title: Administer a cocktail of Ketamine/Xylazine anesthesia i
steps.instruction: Administer a cocktail of Ketamine/Xylazine anesthesia i.p. at 90/10 mg/kg (initial dose) so animal is under deep anesthetic plane. Ketamine/Xylzine will be administered throughout the duration of the procedure at 22.5/2.5 mg/kg for subsequent doses every 30-45min after 1 hour from the initial dose.

steps.title: Remove the body hair from all...
steps.instruction: Remove the body hair from all intended incision sites by shaving.

steps.title: Once the animal is prepped for...
steps.instruction: Once the animal is prepped for surgery and a sterile field has been established, the surgery is ready to begin.

steps.title: Position the animal in supine position...
steps.instruction: Position the animal in supine position on the surgery table with the head away from the surgeon. Provide supplemental warmth during the surgery using heatpad.

steps.title: Loosely tape the animal’s hindlimbs and tail to the table
steps.instruction: Loosely tape the animal’s hindlimbs and tail to the table.

steps.title: Establish a sterile surgical field on...
steps.instruction: Establish a sterile surgical field on the left side of the animal (your right side) and apply sterile draping material. However, because this is a terminal procedure, a sterile field is not necessary.

steps.title: Using small surgical scissors make a 0
steps.instruction: Using small surgical scissors make a 0.5 cm incision parallel to where would the femoral vein would be running in the groin area between the abdomen and thigh.

steps.title: Using the scissors, gently open the...
steps.instruction: Using the scissors, gently open the incision further by blunt dissection to reveal the vasculature.

steps.title: Using hooks or retractors, keep the incision open
steps.instruction: Using hooks or retractors, keep the incision open.

steps.title: Locate the femoral vein, which should...
steps.instruction: Locate the femoral vein, which should be in the center of the incision. The vein should run parallel to the thigh, and there should be a branch coming off it perpendicularly, making a T-shape. Carefully isolate the vein using the fine tip forceps through blunt dissection.

steps.title: Once the vein has been isolated,...
steps.instruction: Once the vein has been isolated, use curved forceps to hook the vein and lift it at a 45 degree angle from the left side. Tie off the vein on the right side with surgical thread. Tie off the branching part of the vein that makes up the bottom of the T-shape using surgical thread.

steps.title: Using a clip, momentarily clip off...
steps.instruction: Using a clip, momentarily clip off the left side of the vein. There now should be a build up of blood in the vein that bubbles up.

steps.title: Prime the P-10 tubing with the...
steps.instruction: Prime the P-10 tubing with the 1:1000 heparin saline solution at the tip and the rest with saline with the tubing attached to a 0.5ml insulin syringe. Using the spring scissors, cut a small incision into the vein and insert the P-10 tubing as your cannula.

steps.title: Remove the clip and insert the...
steps.instruction: Remove the clip and insert the cannula further into the vein.

steps.title: Push the heparin solution in, wait...
steps.instruction: Push the heparin solution in, wait 2-3 seconds and pull back on the syringe. Blood should come up the tubing. There should be no bleeding.

steps.title: The animal is now ready for the MRI protocol
steps.instruction: The animal is now ready for the MRI protocol.
